content: Clinical implications of bioavailability guide route selection and dose adjustment. When switching from IV to oral: calculate oral dose = IV dose / F. For drugs with low/variable bioavailability (e.g., warfarin F~100%, digoxin F~70%, cyclosporine F~30%), therapeutic monitoring is essential. Food effects: high-fat meals increase bioavailability of lipophilic drugs (griseofulvin, atovaquone); decrease it for others (ampicillin, alendronate). Acid suppression reduces absorption of weak bases requiring acidic environment (ketoconazole, atazanavir). Extended-release formulations maintain steady concentrations but have lower peak levels. Sublingual nitroglycerin bypasses first-pass for rapid onset in angina.
